skill.name: seo-leak
skill.description: SEO intelligence engine for competitive analysis, ranking signal assessment, E-E-A-T review, keyword gap research, and audit planning.
skill.summary: Analyze SEO ranking gaps and plan competitive search strategies.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-cryptography alerts are false positives caused by markdown examples and SEO signal notation, not executable code. The skill does describe using external SEO APIs, Google Search Console, browser analysis, analytics platforms, and user-provided URLs, so publication should include a network and data-sharing warning.

medium_findings.title: External SEO Services and Browser Analysis
medium_findings.description: The skill describes integrations with DataForSEO, SEMRush or Ahrefs APIs, Google Search Console, Playwright or Browser, and analytics platforms. This is legitimate for SEO work, but it can disclose user-supplied site URLs, competitor URLs, and analytics context to external services if those integrations are enabled.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The integration list clearly names external APIs, browser page analysis, Google Search Console, and analytics platforms. No malicious endpoint or hidden exfiltration was found, so the concern is controlled network and credential exposure.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Alerts Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick findings point to markdown fenced examples and command-reference templates, not executable scripts. No evidence found of actual shell execution, script files, or command interpolation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown code fences containing prose examples and user prompt templates. The repository scan contains only SKILL.md, so there is no runnable code path for these alerts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are SEO Terminology
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by SEO notation and ordinary audit terms such as Q*, P*, T*, meta descriptions, pricing, and responsive design. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing, encryption, or deprecated algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are SEO documentation and audit checklist text. The terms do not appear in cryptographic functions or executable code.

low_findings.title: User Supplied URLs Need Explicit Scope
low_findings.description: The skill prompts users to provide target sites, competitor URLs, and page URLs for analysis. This is normal SEO behavior, but users should confirm they are allowed to analyze each URL and should avoid sending private analytics data without approval.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL prompts are explicit and useful for SEO analysis, but any browser or API-backed implementation may create external requests. The concern is operational scope, not evidence of malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams often know competitors outrank them but lack a clear reason. This skill structures SEO signal analysis, gap review, and action planning for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Breaks target keywords into query intent, page quality, and trust signal considerations.
Compares pages against top competitors using content, authority, freshness, and format gaps.
Guides E-E-A-T reviews for author expertise, site authority, and trust signals.
Structures SEO audits across technical, content, authority, and prioritization phases.
Creates search strategy recommendations based on current ranking position and timeline.
Documents expected deliverables such as gap reports, 90-day plans, and opportunity rankings.
limitations: It does not include a real-time ranking tracker in the listed Version 1 scope.
It depends on user-provided data or available external SEO tools for live metrics.
It does not automate backlink prospecting or link-building outreach.
It estimates ranking signals and cannot guarantee search engine positions.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Keyword Targets
use_cases.description: Assess whether a keyword is realistic before investing in content production.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Ranking Loss
use_cases.description: Compare a page against competitors to identify content, trust, and intent gaps.

use_cases.title: Plan a Client Audit
use_cases.description: Turn technical, content, and authority checks into a phased audit report.

prompt_templates.title: Check a Keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze whether we should target [keyword]. Our site is [site]. Our current authority and content assets are [context].

prompt_templates.title: Compare Ranking Pages
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a competitive gap analysis for [keyword]. Compare our page [URL] with these competitors: [URLs]. Focus on content, authority, and intent coverage.

prompt_templates.title: Assess E-E-A-T
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate E-E-A-T for [site or page]. Benchmark it against [competitors]. Identify the highest impact trust improvements for the next 90 days.

prompt_templates.title: Build SEO Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 12-month SEO strategy for [business]. Use our current rankings, target keywords, competitor set, content inventory, and authority constraints.

output_examples.input: Should we target a broad software comparison keyword?
output_examples.output: A difficulty assessment with search intent, competitor authority gaps, likely timeline, and safer long-tail alternatives.

output_examples.input: Why does a competitor outrank our page?
output_examples.output: A gap report covering page depth, media formats, freshness, backlinks, trust signals, and prioritized fixes.

output_examples.input: Prepare an SEO audit plan for a client site.
output_examples.output: A phased audit outline with technical checks, content analysis, authority review, deliverables, and a 90-day action plan.

best_practices: Provide current ranking data, target markets, and competitor URLs before requesting recommendations.
Separate confirmed metrics from estimates when presenting findings to stakeholders.
Get approval before using external APIs, browser tools, or private analytics data.
anti_patterns: Do not treat estimated ranking signals as exact Google scoring data.
Do not request analysis of private analytics exports without permission.
Do not use the recommendations as a promise of ranking results.

faq.question: Does this skill track rankings in real time?
faq.answer: No. The listed Version 1 scope does not include real-time ranking tracking.

faq.question: Can it compare my page with competitors?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides a framework for content, authority, technical, and E-E-A-T comparison.

faq.question: Does it require external SEO tools?
faq.answer: Not for planning. Live metrics require approved data from SEO tools, browser analysis, or analytics platforms.

faq.question: Can it guarantee a ranking improvement?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured recommendations, but search rankings depend on many external factors.

faq.question: Is it suitable for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide research, audits, and documentation workflows in Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What data should I prepare?
faq.answer: Prepare target keywords, current pages, competitor URLs, ranking data, content inventory, and approved analytics context.
